text:
UCI ICS Faculty
Professor Richard Klefstad
Dr. Klefstad teaches ICS 46: Data Structures and is known for his rigorous approach to teaching algorithms. He has extensive experience in systems programming and compiler design.
Research interests: Operating systems, real-time systems, embedded systems.
Email: klefstad@uci.edu
Professor David Kay
Research interests: Computer science education, software engineering practices.
Email: kay@uci.edu

ucation, software engineering practices.
Email: kay@uci.edu
Professor Michael Goodrich
Dr. Goodrich is a prominent researcher in computational geometry and algorithms. He teaches advanced courses in data structures. His research has significant contributions to algorithm design and analysis.
Research interests: Computational geometry, graph algorithms, security.
Email: goodrich@uci.edu
Professor Thornton

ithms, security.
Email: goodrich@uci.edu
Professor Thornton
Research interests: Education, curriculum design.
Email: thornton@uci.edu
Professor Pattis
Professor Pattis is known for teaching ICS 21 with emphasis on software engineering principles. He has contributed significantly to computer science education at UCI.
Research interests: Software engineering, education.
Email: pattis@uci.edu
